About Me

I like to build.

Hey, Thanks for stopping by! I'm a student at [Your University] pursuing a degree in [Your Major]. I'm passionate about technology and building things that make a difference.

I'm originally from [Your City]. As a child I loved to build entire empires from legos. I now have a passion for building things a bit more intricate, like websites & apps, robots, and a startup.

I'm also interested in research, especially in Machine Learning, Large Language Models, and AI. You can find my papers here.

In my spare time, I enjoy reading, exploring, playing sports, competitive programming, playing the guitar, and cooking.

editable-section-about-section:
I like to build.

Hey, thanks for stopping by! I'm a student at the University of Texas at Austin pursuing a degree in Electrical and Computer Engineering. I'm passionate about technology and building things that make a difference!

I'm originally from Normal, Illinois. As a child I loved to build entire empires from Legos. Now, I have a passion for creating things a bit more intricate, like websites, apps, robots, PCB/chip design, and laser-printed and 3D-printed personal projects.

I'm also interested in research, especially in Information Security, Large Language Models, and Anomaly Detection. One cool fact is that I coded the editor to create this website and fully used that to dynamically and aesthetically lay everything out!

In my spare time, I enjoy bouldering, working out, sand volleyball, cooking, coding projects, and trying new foods!

editable-section-project-detail-lc3-asse:
Back to Projects
View Code
Systems

LC-3 Assembler & Simulator

Systems Engineer • 2024

About the Project

Developed a complete LC-3 assembler and simulator in C as part of ECE 306 (Intro to Computing) at UT Austin. The assembler performs a two-pass translation of LC-3 assembly source files into machine code, resolving labels and generating binary output compatible with the LC-3 ISA. The simulator then executes that machine code, faithfully modeling the LC-3 datapath including the register file, ALU, condition codes (N/Z/P), memory-mapped I/O, and a custom microsequencer with a full state diagram governing instruction fetch, decode, and execute cycles. Labs covered I/O programming, subroutines, the stack, and interrupt-driven I/O.

Timeline

1 semester

Year

2024

Key Result

Full ISA coverage

Technologies Used

CLC-3 AssemblyMicrosequencerISA Design

editable-section-project-detail-medical-:
Back to Projects
View Code
AI/ML

Indian Recipe AI

Python Developer • 2025

About the Project

Built a conversational AI chatbot--ChatMasala--that generates detailed Indian vegetarian curry recipes using OpenAI's language model APIs. The system handles natural language queries about ingredients, dietary restrictions, and cooking methods, returning culturally accurate recipes with structured ingredient lists and step-by-step instructions. Integrated with React for a responsive chat UI and Firebase for session persistence, achieving sub-2-second response times across 100+ recipe interactions.

Timeline

6 months

Year

2025

Key Result

100+ recipes generated

Technologies Used

PythonOpenAI APIReactFirebase
